scope_note: QA Phasing In: Odd-Wave Half-Integer Half-Cycle Structure cert [331]. Primary source: Iverson, B. (1991) Quantum Arithmetic Volume II -- Books 3 & 4: New Wave Theory / Synchronous Harmonics. Delta Spectrum Research, La Junta CO. Book 3 pp.8, 11-12: 'PHASING IN', 'OTHER CYCLES'. Iverson discusses whether waves can synchronize at a half-cycle point. For period p, the half-cycle is at p/2; if p is odd, p/2 is not an integer -- no discrete half-cycle sync exists. (C1) Odd periods {3,5,7,9,11,13,15}: 2*floor(p/2) != p -- no integer half-cycle. (C2) Even periods {2,4,6,8,10,12}: 2*(p//2) == p -- exact integer half-cycle. (C3) Coprime odd pairs: lcm(p,q)=p*q (no shared 2-factor); lcm itself odd -- no integer half-cycle at full sync either. (C4) Par-type partition: 2-par (n mod 4==2) and 4-par (n mod 4==0) have integer half-cycles; 3-par (n mod 4==3) and 5-par (n mod 4==1) do not. Connects cert [326] par-type structure. (C5) For odd p: no k in {1..p-1} satisfies 2k=p (half-cycle sync impossible before full period). Builds on certs [325] (to-be-prime) and [326] (four par types). DOI: primary text, no external DOI.
